title: ranunculus trichophyllus var. capillaceus
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus var. capillaceus, also known as creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory agent and a treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of creeping buttercup include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating minor injuries and skin irritations. Creeping buttercup can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of applications.
